summary: Iteration 5 — library-swap verification on the Expo Go web preview. PACKAGING is good (react-native-bluetooth-classic@1.73.0-rc.17 + with-rn-bluetooth-classic@1.0.5 installed; old react-native-bluetooth-escpos-printer removed; buffer@6.0.3 resolvable; app.json plugin block correct; android perms + iOS NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription present; Expo bundle compiles cleanly with no red-screen). RUNTIME FLOW is BROKEN on the web preview: the new printer.ts native-availability check is too loose — `typeof RNBluetoothClassic.getBondedDevices === 'function'` is TRUE on web because the library ships a JS BluetoothModule class whose methods always exist regardless of native linkage. Consequence: isNativePrinterAvailable() returns true on web → printer-setup banner shows 'Scanning nearby Bluetooth thermal printers (58mm).' instead of the expected 'Demo mode (Expo Go preview)…' → scanPrinters() invokes the real getBondedDevices() which internally calls this._nativeModule.getBondedDevices() with this._nativeModule === undefined → user sees the red error string 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading getBondedDevices)' rendered on screen and the empty 'No printers found' state. Demo devices DEMO-58-01 and DEMO-POS-58 NEVER render. Acceptance step 'printer-connect-DEMO-58-01' is therefore UNREACHABLE — the whole acceptance flow fails at the first interactive step. Confirmed reproduction at 375x667 on https://sticky-delight-app.preview.emergentagent.com/printer-setup. The same bug will reproduce on a physical phone running Expo Go (Expo Go does not bundle this third-party native module), so the user's planned 'rebuild the APK with a known-good codebase' is blocked — Expo Go preview is no longer usable. Downstream verification via the 'Skip for now' link: /home loads, order-number-value='#1', generate-button fires POST to https://expertdevelopers.in/generate-8858-quote-for-sfj (CORS-blocked on web as documented), /preview opens with order_number=1 in the URL, BUT preview-quote never populates due to CORS so the print-button is not rendered and preview-toast cannot be exercised on web. None of those downstream pieces can be considered verified for this iteration because the entry-point (demo-mode detection) is wrong. Deprecated packages detected: none. SafeAreaView issues detected: none. Detection-logic issue detected in /app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts:loadNative() — needs to gate on NativeModules.RNBluetoothClassic instead of typeof method check.

ui_bugs.issue: Banner text is 'Scanning nearby Bluetooth thermal printers (58mm).' on the web preview instead of the expected 'Demo mode (Expo Go preview). Real Bluetooth printing activates in the built APK.' Root cause: isNativePrinterAvailable() returns true when it should return false on web/Expo Go.

Demo devices DEMO-58-01 and DEMO-POS-58 do NOT render. printer-list test-id never mounts because devices.length===0 after the failed scan. printer-connect-DEMO-58-01 and printer-connect-DEMO-POS-58 are not reachable. A visible red error string 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading getBondedDevices)' is rendered below 'No printers found'.

integration_issues.flow: Printer scan in Expo Go / web preview
integration_issues.issue: react-native-bluetooth-classic exposes a JS BluetoothModule class with getBondedDevices() as a real function even when NativeModules.RNBluetoothClassic is undefined. printer.ts only checks `typeof getBondedDevices === 'function'`, so the simulated fallback never engages and the very first scan call throws a TypeError that surfaces in the UI. The same failure will reproduce on a real phone running Expo Go.

Not verifiable on web because the preceding CORS block on https://expertdevelopers.in/generate-8858-quote-for-sfj prevents preview-quote from populating, which gates the print-button. This is a documented browser-only artifact (per iter_2/4), not a regression — but it means the new printer.printReceipt() simulated path COULD NOT BE EXERCISED in this iteration. Worth flagging because, even if reachable, printReceipt() would currently route through the (broken) native adapter and call activeDevice.write() with activeDevice===null → a second 'Printer not connected' error, since the connect() flow is also gated behind the broken scan.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning carried over from iter_2/3/4: 'TouchableWithoutFeedback is deprecated. Please use Pressable.' Non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/app/home.tsx:12,86
Console warning carried over: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/app/preview.tsx:275-279
Console warning carried over: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/app/preview.tsx:215

action_items: FIX printer.ts native-detection (CRITICAL). Replace the `typeof RNBluetoothClassic.getBondedDevices !== 'function'` guard at /app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts:42 with a check on the actual native module presence. Minimal patch: at the top of loadNative(), import `NativeModules` from 'react-native' and short-circuit `if (!NativeModules?.RNBluetoothClassic) return null;` BEFORE the require/typeof checks (or right after the require). That way the require() can keep its try/catch wrapper (some bundlers may still throw), but the more robust gate is the absence of the native bridge — true both on web preview and inside Expo Go on a phone. Once gated correctly, isNativePrinterAvailable() will return false in Expo Go → banner switches to 'Demo mode' → simulatedScan() returns DEMO-58-01 + DEMO-POS-58 → acceptance flow proceeds.

ADD `buffer` to /app/frontend/package.json `dependencies` (currently used by /app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts:13 but only transitively present in node_modules — pinning it explicitly avoids a future hoist break). Suggested: `"buffer": "^6.0.3"`.

After the fix, RE-RUN the full acceptance flow on https://sticky-delight-app.preview.emergentagent.com/printer-setup at 375x667 to verify: (a) demo banner text contains 'Demo mode', (b) printer-connect-DEMO-58-01 mounts and is tappable, (c) /home order-number-value='#1' after connect, (d) generate→/preview→print-button→preview-toast 'Printed (simulated)...', (e) back to /home shows order #2, (f) /settings printer-row shows the demo device + settings-disconnect works.

OPTIONAL polish carried from prior iterations (still non-blocking): swap TouchableWithoutFeedback → Pressable on home.tsx, swap shadow* → boxShadow on preview.tsx noteShadow, move pointerEvents from prop to style on preview-toast.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts:37-44 — loadNative() availability gate is INSUFFICIENT. The library being imported here (react-native-bluetooth-classic) ships a JS BluetoothModule class whose `getBondedDevices` is always typeof 'function' regardless of whether the native bridge is linked. The current guard therefore passes on web and inside Expo Go, and the resulting adapter throws a TypeError on first invocation. Correct check is `NativeModules.RNBluetoothClassic` presence from 'react-native'.

/app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts:13 — uses `import { Buffer } from 'buffer'` but `buffer` is NOT declared in /app/frontend/package.json dependencies. It currently resolves via a transitive hoist; declaring it explicitly is safer.

/app/frontend/app.json:40-58 — plugin block for `with-rn-bluetooth-classic` is correctly an array with peripheralUsageDescription + alwaysUsageDescription; android permissions and iOS NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription are present. No issues here.

/app/frontend/package.json:42,51 — react-native-bluetooth-classic@^1.73.0-rc.17 and with-rn-bluetooth-classic@^1.0.5 are installed and the deprecated react-native-bluetooth-escpos-printer is removed. Packaging is clean.

success_rate.frontend: 2/5 acceptance criteria pass. Criterion 1 (package.json contents) PASS. Criterion 2 (app.json + permissions) PASS. Criterion 3 (Expo bundle compiles, no red screen) PASS but with a runtime TypeError surfaced in the UI text. Criterion 4 (end-to-end demo-mode flow) FAIL — demo banner missing, demo devices missing, native-detection bug blocks the entire scan→connect→home→preview→print flow. Criterion 5 (/settings printer-row + settings-disconnect) NOT VERIFIED — gated behind a successful connect that this iteration could not complete.

context_for_next_testing_agent: The library swap to react-native-bluetooth-classic + with-rn-bluetooth-classic introduced a regression in the demo-mode detection logic in /app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts. The library exposes a JS-only class that LOOKS LIKE the native adapter, so the typeof-function check at line 42 always passes on web and in Expo Go. Need to gate on `NativeModules.RNBluetoothClassic` from 'react-native' (which is undefined unless the user is running a custom dev-client or a built APK). Once that gate is in place, the simulated fallback will engage and all the testIDs from iter_4 (printer-connect-DEMO-58-01, generate-button, preview-quote, print-button, preview-toast, settings-disconnect, etc.) should pass exactly as they did in iter_4 (the simulated code paths were verified working in iter_2/3/4). After E1 applies the fix, retest the full acceptance flow at 375x667 viewport — should take ~2 minutes. CORS on expertdevelopers.in remains a known web-only artifact and is expected to keep blocking the preview-quote populate step on web, but that's accepted by the spec.
rca_of_issue: Symptom: /printer-setup on https://sticky-delight-app.preview.emergentagent.com renders banner 'Scanning nearby Bluetooth thermal printers (58mm).' (native mode) instead of 'Demo mode (Expo Go preview)…' and shows a visible error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading getBondedDevices)' with no demo devices listed. Reproduction: open the URL in any browser (or Expo Go on a phone) at 375x667 → wait ~3s. Root cause: /app/frontend/src/lib/printer.ts:37-44 loadNative() decides the native adapter is available based on `typeof RNBluetoothClassic.getBondedDevices === 'function'`. The library ships /app/frontend/node_modules/react-native-bluetooth-classic/lib/BluetoothModule.js whose class always defines getBondedDevices (line 101) and forwards to `this._nativeModule.getBondedDevices()` (line 103). Because Expo Go and the web preview do not bundle the native side of this third-party module, `this._nativeModule` is undefined, so the call throws TypeError. The wrapper in printer.ts:83 awaits getBondedDevices() and the error propagates up to printer-setup.tsx where it is caught into scanError state and rendered as the red string at the bottom of the screen. Mitigation: in printer.ts:loadNative(), import `NativeModules` from 'react-native' and add `if (!NativeModules?.RNBluetoothClassic) return null;` as the first guard. That returns null on web + Expo Go, isNativePrinterAvailable() becomes false, the banner switches to the Demo string, and simulatedScan()/simulated connect/simulated print all engage as designed.
